lint.command: ROOT=/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control && cd "$ROOT" && bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh && bash ci/check-architecture.sh && bash ci/check-enterprise-policy.sh && bash ci/check-orchestrator-layer.sh && python3 scripts/check_flaky_tags.py --tests-root erp-domain/src/test/java --gate gate-fast && bash scripts/guard_openapi_contract_drift.sh

blockingIssues.description: AccountingCoreEngineCore still centralizes canonical accounting behavior as an ~8100-line monolith, including createStandardJournal/createJournalEntry, payroll posting/payment, and dealer/supplier settlement flows, so decomposition into focused canonical owners was not achieved.

SettlementService and AccountingIdempotencyService still preserve the prior delegation chain into inherited AccountingCoreEngineCore settlement implementations, so focused settlement ownership remains unresolved.
PayrollAccountingService is still a thin wrapper over inherited monolith methods (`super.postPayrollRun` / `super.recordPayrollPayment`) instead of owning migrated payroll behavior.

Manual-journal replay now performs a single immediate reserved-row lookup after reservation collision, so concurrent duplicate submissions can fail with INTERNAL_CONCURRENCY_FAILURE before the leader transaction commits instead of replaying the canonical journal result.

The same single-read replay regression affects reservation-first receipt/payment/settlement/correction paths, where follower requests can throw missing-reservation failures before the leader writes journal/allocation rows.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit decomposition verification checkpoint that rejects wrapper-only refactors: focused services must own migrated business logic rather than inheriting or delegating back to AccountingCoreEngineCore/super methods.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The accounting-core-decomposition-foundation review found wrapper shells deleted but core methods still centralized in AccountingCoreEngineCore, with SettlementService and PayrollAccountingService forwarding through inheritance/dependency hops instead of becoming canonical owners.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Adjust or conditionalize Step 6 remediation-log requirements so workers are not instructed to update `.factory/library/remediation-log.md` when higher-priority packet rules explicitly forbid docs/library edits.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The accounting-core-decomposition-foundation handoff recorded a justified procedure deviation because the skill required a remediation-log append while the packet instructions prohibited doc updates unless explicitly requested.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the clean-worktree fallback for shared dirty workspaces, including when to use isolated worktrees and how to complete/hand off a clean feature commit without touching unrelated local edits.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both accounting-write-contract-and-replay-hardening and accounting-consumer-proof-and-duplicate-seam-cleanup reported procedure ambiguity around pre-existing dirty files; one worker created an isolated worktree to keep its commit clean, and another explicitly flagged worker-base guidance as underspecified for successful completion with unrelated `.factory/droids/*.md` edits present.

rejectedObservations.observation: The canonical `targeted-accounting-proof` pack still lacks a direct duplicate-replay contention probe for the reserved-row accounting write paths changed in bee7f023.
rejectedObservations.reason: not-applied: this would require new contention tests or a new proof command first, so it cannot be safely handled as an additive services.yaml/library update from review evidence alone.
